items.title: users.is_internal backfilled correctly
items.precondition: Migration 20260701010000_add_is_internal_to_users.ts has run on the target database.
steps: 1. Run: SELECT email, is_internal FROM users WHERE is_internal = true ORDER BY email;
2. Confirm the result set is exactly the @getalloro.com accounts and nothing else.
items.notes: Not run — migration has not been applied to dev/prod yet.

items.title: Pilot-session rows purged from app_usage_events
steps: 1. Before migrating, run: SELECT count(*) FROM app_usage_events WHERE is_pilot_session = true;
2. After migrating, run the same query.
items.notes: Not run — migration has not been applied to dev/prod yet.

items.title: Alloro Teams org and internal users excluded from every telemetry metric
items.precondition: T1 migration applied; an @getalloro.com account has logged into the app after this deploy.
steps: 1. GET /api/admin/mission-control/telemetry?range=30d as a super admin.
2. Confirm the Alloro Teams org does not appear in organizationUsage.
3. Confirm summary.activeUsers / totalSessions / totalActiveMinutes do not include internal accounts (cross-check against a known internal-only session).
items.expected: No is_sandbox org or is_internal user activity appears in summary, dailyUsage, surfaceUsage, pageUsage, organizationUsage, or per-user detail views.
items.notes: Requires dev deploy + live traffic to verify meaningfully.

items.title: Telemetry write-side block: internal/sandbox/pilot events are not recorded
items.precondition: T1, T3, T4 code deployed; test account available at an @getalloro.com email.
steps: 1. Log in as an @getalloro.com account, navigate several pages.
2. Query app_usage_events for that user_id and created_at after login time.
3. Repeat with a real (non-internal) client account as a control.
items.expected: Zero new rows for the internal account; normal rows for the control account.
items.notes: Requires dev deploy and an interactive login — left for human/computer-use run.

items.title: Low-engagement flag surfaces zero-activity and low-activity client orgs
items.precondition: T3, T5 deployed; at least one eligible client org with zero or <5 min activity in the trailing 30 days.
steps: 1. GET /api/admin/mission-control/telemetry?range=30d.
2. Confirm organizationUsage includes an entry with isLowEngagement: true for a known low-activity org (e.g. Garrison on prod).
3. Confirm a zero-activity org appears even though it has no app_usage_events rows at all.
items.expected: Low-activity and zero-activity eligible client orgs are present and flagged.
items.notes: Requires dev/prod data to verify meaningfully — dev may not have a naturally zero-activity eligible org.

items.title: Pilot toggle removed; low-engagement badge renders
items.precondition: Frontend deployed; admin session in Mission Control.
steps: 1. Open Mission Control → Telemetry tab.
2. Confirm only the Admin toggle remains (no Pilot toggle).
3. Confirm a flagged org shows the "Low engagement" badge next to its name.
items.expected: Pilot toggle absent; badge visible on flagged rows.
items.notes: Not run — needs an authenticated Mission Control session; left for the user to verify in their own browser tab per established workflow.

items.title: DentalEMR archived and off the default org list
steps: 1. Archive DentalEMR (org id 6) via the existing Archive action.
2. Confirm it no longer appears in the default (active) organization list.
3. Confirm organizations.archived_at is set for org id 6.
items.expected: DentalEMR archived, absent from default views.
items.notes: Manual operator action — not part of this code execution.

items.title: Updated telemetry numbers shared with Jo and Corey
items.precondition: T1-T6 deployed and verified on prod.
steps: 1. Pull the post-fix telemetry summary.
2. Share with Jo and Corey.
items.notes: Post-deploy communication action, tracked here per the card's Definition of Done.
